read_only: Read,Glob,Grep,Bash(git status *),Bash(git branch *),Bash(git log *),Bash(git show *),Bash(git diff *),Bash(rg *),Bash(Get-Content *)

claude.notes: Use the .cmd shim on Windows. The cheap tier is explicit Haiku/low effort; Haiku 4.5 is Anthropic's current cost-efficient model. Read-only mode disables Edit/Write and allows only focused inspection commands.

codex.notes: Re-enabled 2026-07-11: CLI 0.144.1 runs the configured gpt-5.6-sol model (verified live; the 0.135.0 incompatibility from the 2026-07-10 health check is resolved). Account-level usage limits can still fail a run — the ChatGPT-account quota is shared with Hermes, and the error surfaces in the dispatch log with a retry time.

hermes.notes: Hermes is fueled by the same Codex/ChatGPT account as the codex worker, so a Codex usage-limit outage blocks Hermes too. Verify cost/provider settings before long loops.

cursor.notes: Cursor is kept as a manual IDE lane because the installed CLI is not a clean stdout worker by default.

antigravity.notes: Antigravity CLI (agy 1.0.13, verified live 2026-07-11 via --print). Flags must precede the prompt (Go-style flag parsing). --sandbox restricts terminal use but is not a hard no-write guarantee, so keep read_only briefs explicit about not editing; write mode needs --dangerously-skip-permissions or headless runs hang on permission prompts. Default --print-timeout is 5m. Default model is Gemini 3.5 Flash (Medium); tier routing uses the display-name strings from `agy models` and the --model override was verified live (3.1 Pro (High) self-reported and confirmed in the session log). Gemini 3.1 Pro (Low) exists as a middle option if standard needs more depth. The Antigravity IDE remains available as a separate manual/visual lane.
